Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to act as a target LLM that analyzes the provided event log data for anomalies and undesirable behaviors hypothesize reasons and propose SQL queries to investigate further

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to analyze the provided event logs for cases 1001 1002 1003 and 1004 against the assumed normal process flow 1 Register Order Sales 2 Perform Credit Check Finance 3 Validate Stock Warehouse 4 Confirm Shipment Logistics 5 Ship Goods Logistics 6 Issue Invoice Finance 7 Receive Payment Finance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment This follows the normal flow perfectly No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Register Order event 8 Confirm Shipment event 9 ANOMALY This happens before Credit Check and Validate Stock Ship Goods event 10 ANOMALY Happens before Credit Check and Validate Stock and before Validate Stock entirely Perform Credit Check event 11 Validate Stock event 12 ANOMALY Happens after shipping Issue Invoice event 13 Receive Payment event 14 Additional info attempted early Y on Confirm Shipment suggests someone knew this was early wrong This is a major process violation Shipping before credit check and stock validation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods ANOMALY Missing Validate Stock and Confirm Shipment before shipping Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment ANOMALY Happens after shipping late confirmation Y confirms this Receive Payment Missing Validate Stock activity entirely Shipment confirmed after goods already shipped

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Register Order Receive Payment ANOMALY Payment received at the very beginning before anything else Issue Invoice ANOMALY Invoice issued after payment reverse order Confirm Shipment shipment scheduled N maybe cancelled or failed Ship Goods Missing Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock entirely Payment received before credit check stock validation or even shipping invoice in correct order

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now let me categorize the anomalies Case 1002 Skipped steps Shipping occurred before Credit Check and Stock Validation Out of order Confirm Shipment happened before prerequisites Risk Shipped goods without verifying creditworthiness or stock availability Case 1003 Missing activity No Validate Stock recorded Out of order Confirm Shipment happened after Ship Goods late confirmation Risk Shipped without confirming shipment plan possibly without stock check Case 1004 Missing activities No Credit Check No Stock Validation Out of order Payment received before Invoice issued pre payment and before any fulfillment steps Possible prepayment scenario but missing critical risk assessment steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypotheses for why these occur System errors Automated workflows triggering steps prematurely Policy violations Staff bypassing controls for priority customers note case 1002 is priority type Training issues Staff not following proper sequence Customer pressure Priority customers demanding faster processing Process variations Pre payment scenarios case 1004 or drop shipping scenarios Missing data logging Stock validation might happen but not be logged though we should check Fraud Bypassing credit checks for certain customers

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Case 1002 early shipping Check if this is related to order type priority Check which resources were involved and their roles departments Check timestamps to see how rushed it was Check if there are other cases with similar attempted early flags For Case 1003 missing validation Check if Validate Stock is truly missing or just not logged Check if the resource WarehouseClerk 3 has a pattern of skipping steps Check if Confirm Shipment late is a pattern For Case 1004 pre payment Check if Receive Payment first is common for high value orders 3000 00 Check customer history customer id 504 Check if Credit Check was done but not logged or if there is a policy for certain customers General queries Find all cases where activity sequences violate the expected order Find cases missing critical activities Credit Check Validate Stock Correlate with order value high value orders skipping checks Correlate with order type priority vs standard Check resource patterns specific SalesReps or WarehouseClerks involved in anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me structure the response 1 Anomaly Analysis case by case 2 Hypotheses why these happen 3 SQL Investigation Queries to validate hypotheses I should write the SQL for PostgreSQL specifically since that is the database type Key SQL patterns to use Window functions LEAD LAG to check activity ordering Self joins or window functions to check if activities are missing Joins with orders and resources tables Timestamp comparisons Pattern matching on additional info

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also note subtle things Case 1002 has attempted early Y in additional info for Confirm Shipment this suggests the system flagged it Case 1003 has late confirmation Y for Confirm Shipment Case 1004 has shipment scheduled N suggesting the confirmation was negative cancelled These additional info fields are important clues

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final check The user wants the LLM to identify anomalies hypothesize why and propose SQL queries without hints So I should present this as if I am discovering these patterns myself Structure 1 Executive Summary of Findings 2 Detailed Anomaly Analysis by case and pattern type 3 Root Cause Hypotheses 4 Investigative SQL Queries with explanations of what they find This looks complete
